structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Start/Stop
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU supply fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan runs during Warmup Mode, Setback Mode, and Occupied Mode when there are heating-fan requests, with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System is in Warmup Mode, Setback Mode, or Occupied Mode with heating-fan requests
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan shall run with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, and during Occupied Mode while there are any heating-fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.

evidence_quote: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, and during Occupied Mode while there are any heating-fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
evidence_text: Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, and during Occupied Mode while there are any heating-fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Totalize Airflow from VAV Boxes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point for display, diagnostics, and filter alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System is operating (optional, if no AFMS)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Sum VAV box airflow rates to obtain overall supply air rate without an AFMS at the air-handler discharge.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air-handler discharge. This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.

evidence_quote: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air-handler discharge. This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.
evidence_text: Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air-handler discharge. This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using T&R Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU supply fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Static pressure setpoint is reset using Trim & Respond logic with parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset static pressure setpoint using T&R logic with specified parameters.
configurable_values: R: Zone hot-duct static pressure reset requests
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

evidence_quote: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.
evidence_text: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control loop is enabled when supply air fan is proven ON, disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable control loop; if disabled, set output to zero.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Control loop is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.

evidence_quote: Control loop is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
evidence_text: Control loop is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Reset during Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, SAT setpoint is reset using T&R logic between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset SAT setpoint using T&R logic with parameters in Table 5.17.2.2.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.

evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.
evidence_text: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Setpoint during Warmup and Setback Modes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, SAT setpoint is Max_HtgSAT.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode. Setpoint shall be Max_HtgSAT.

evidence_quote: During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode. Setpoint shall be Max_HtgSAT.
evidence_text: During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode. Setpoint shall be Max_HtgSAT.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Control via PID Loop
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Supply air temperature is maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Maintain SAT at setpoint by modulating heating coil valve.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Supply air temperature shall be maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil.

evidence_quote: Supply air temperature shall be maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil.
evidence_text: Supply air temperature shall be maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Interval Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU supply fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Level 4 alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours; reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm; reset counter on acknowledgment.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
evidence_text: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU supply fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm when status differs from command for 15 seconds: Level 2 if commanded ON but status OFF; Level 4 if commanded OFF but status ON.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate alarm: Level 2 for commanded ON/status OFF; Level 4 for commanded OFF/status ON.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
evidence_text: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Level 4 alarm when filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 minutes and airflow >20% of design. Alarm limit varies with airflow: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Filter DP > max(alarm limit, 12.5 Pa) for 10 minutes and airflow >20% of design
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm. Alarm limit varies with airflow per formula.
configurable_values: DP100 (filter high limit at design airflow)
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Filter DP > max(DPx, 12.5 Pa) for 10 minutes with airflow >20%
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when airflow (expressed as a percentage of design airflow or design speed if total airflow is not known) exceeds 20% : Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with total airflow (if available; use fan speed if total airflow is not known) as follows: DPx=DP100(x)1.4

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Duct Static Pressure Too Low with Fan at Full Speed (FC#1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when duct static pressure average is below setpoint minus threshold and VFD speed is at or near maximum.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: DSP_avg < DSPSP - εDSP AND VFD speed ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault condition with possible diagnoses: problem with VFD, mechanical problem with fan, fan undersized, SAT setpoint too high.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: DSP_avg < DSPSP - εDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Description Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed Possible Diagnosis Problem with VFD Mechanical problem with fan Fan undersized SAT Setpoint too high (too much zone demand)

evidence_quote: FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Description Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed Possible Diagnosis Problem with VFD Mechanical problem with fan Fan undersized SAT Setpoint too high (too much zone demand)
evidence_text: FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Description Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed Possible Diagnosis Problem with VFD Mechanical problem with fan Fan undersized SAT Setpoint too high (too much zone demand)

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: SAT Too Low in Full Heating (FC#2)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when supply air temperature average is below setpoint minus threshold and heating coil valve is at or near 100%.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault condition with possible diagnoses: SAT sensor error, heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, fouled or undersized coil, HW temperature too low or unavailable, gas/electric heat unavailable.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#2 Equation SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Description SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized heating coil HW temperature too low or HW unavailable Gas or electric heat unavailable

evidence_quote: FC#2 Equation SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Description SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized heating coil HW temperature too low or HW unavailable Gas or electric heat unavailable
evidence_text: FC#2 Equation SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Description SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized heating coil HW temperature too low or HW unavailable Gas or electric heat unavailable

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil (FC#3)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when return air minus supply air temperature exceeds combined error threshold plus fan heat, and heating coil valve is at 0%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: RAT_avg - SAT_avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT² + εRAT²) + ΔTSF AND HC = 0%
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault condition with possible diagnoses: HCET sensor error, HCLT sensor error, heating coil valve stuck open or leaking, gas/electric heat stuck on.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: RAT_avg - SAT_avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT² + εRAT²) + ΔTSF and HC = 0%
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#3 Equation RAT AVG - SAT AVG ≥ √ƐSAT2+ ƐRAT2 + TSF and HC = 0 % Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis HCET sensor error HCLT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking Gas or electric heat stuck on

evidence_quote: FC#3 Equation RAT AVG - SAT AVG ≥ √ƐSAT2+ ƐRAT2 + TSF and HC = 0 % Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis HCET sensor error HCLT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking Gas or electric heat stuck on
evidence_text: FC#3 Equation RAT AVG - SAT AVG ≥ √ƐSAT2+ ƐRAT2 + TSF and HC = 0 % Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis HCET sensor error HCLT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking Gas or electric heat stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when AHU is not operating and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU not operating OR mode change of any Zone Group
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend AFDD evaluation for ModeDelay minutes after mode change.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU

evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU
evidence_text: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Alarm Delay for Fault Reporting
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition must be continuously TRUE for AlarmDelay minutes before being reported to the operator.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.
evidence_text: A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Test Mode for Instant Testing
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode temporarily sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.
evidence_text: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD: Fault Condition Alarm Level and Content
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a fault condition is reported, it shall be a Level 3 alarm including description and possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 3 alarm with description and possible diagnoses.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5.

evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5.
evidence_text: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5.
